behavior_summary: U8P_CLRLAVA::gotHit is a guarded hit-handling process that responds when the object/NPC is struck. It prevents reentrancy (via butthead and neverSinks), inspects the hit source (param1), and branches on both param1 and the item's frame to either play a first-hit SFX/spawn a sprite and apply small randomized damage, destroy the source item, or run a forced-kill animation/sequence on an NPC.

When forcing an NPC death the process sets avatar stasis, sets the butthead guard, repositions the NPC (Item::move), resets kernel refs (Kernel::resetRef), runs an NPC animation (Npc::doAnim), ensures the NPC is killed by clearing protective flags and calling Item::receiveHit with the NPC's HP, then restores the previous dead/alive marker if needed.
Visual and audio feedback are coordinated (playSFX, createSprite) and some actions occur only once per-hit via temporary local flags to avoid duplicate effects.
notable_control_flow: Entry guarded by global/local flags: skip if neverSinks present or butthead indicates an ongoing sequence.
Primary branching on param1 (hit-source/selector) and on Item::getFrame(this) <= 0x20 to choose damage/destroy/forced-kill behavior.
First-hit sub-branch: playSFX + createSprite at coordinates read from param1 (Item::getX/Y/Z), tracked by a local flag to avoid repeats.
Damage branch: call Item::receiveHit with a small randomized amount (FREE::randomInRange) and direction computed by Item::getDirToItem.

Forced-kill branch (NPC): set avatar stasis, set butthead, move and animate the NPC, clear immortal/withstand flags, call Item::receiveHit with full HP to guarantee death, then possibly restore prior dead state.
open_questions: What exact values of param1 correspond to each branch (the metadata treats param1 as a selector/hit-source but its precise semantics are unclear).
What does Kernel::resetRef operate on (which kernel reference id) and why is it toggled around the move/animation sequence?
What is the intended purpose of the spawned auxiliary process (spawn -> FREE::func1C19) near the end of the sequence?
Are there any global flags beyond butthead and neverSinks that influence this behavior in other parts of the code base (not present in this excerpt)?
Is the frame threshold (<= 0x20) semantically a type/category test (e.g., breakable vs. durable) or an animation-state cutoff?

main_function.name: U8P_CLRLAVA::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Handle a 'got hit' event on this item/NPC: play first-hit effects, apply damage or destroy the hit source, or run a forced-kill/animation sequence on an NPC while preventing reentrancy and preserving prior alive/dead state where possible.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine as a 'got hit' process for this object/NPC.
Proceeds only when a reentry guard is not set (butthead not active) and neverSinks is not set.
Behavior further depends on the value of the first parameter (param1) and the current Item::getFrame(this) value.

external_calls.purpose: Read the item's frame/index to select behavior branch (frame <= 0x20 changes handling).
Read X coordinate of the hit source for effect placement / comparisons.
Read Y coordinate of the hit source for effect placement / comparisons.
Read Z (level) coordinate of the hit source for effect placement / comparisons.
Play a sound effect when a first-hit visual occurs for audio feedback.
Spawn a visual effect sprite (explosion/hit) at computed coordinates to show impact.
Apply damage to the item/NPC (used both for small randomized damage and for a full-HP hit to guarantee kill).
Generate a small random damage amount used when applying variable damage.
Compute direction from this object toward the hit source for receiveHit direction parameter.
Destroy/remove the hit source item in branches where destruction is required (param1 indicates destruction).
Temporarily freeze the avatar (likely to preserve camera/animation state) during the forced-kill sequence.
Reset kernel references/flags around the move/animation sequence (system bookkeeping), exact ref id uncertain.
Reposition the NPC/item for the animation/kill sequence or to remove it by moving to (0,0,0).
Spawn/queue an auxiliary process near the end of the sequence; exact effect unclear.
Run an NPC animation as part of the forced-kill/impact sequence.
Check if NPC was already dead to preserve/restore original state after forced-kill.
Mark NPC dead as part of the forced-kill sequence.
Restore the NPC's dead marker if it was not originally dead before the forced-kill.
Clear immortal flag so receiveHit can actually kill the NPC.
Clear any 'withstand death' protection so the forced-kill is effective.
Obtain NPC current HP so receiveHit can be invoked with a value guaranteeing death.
Adjust camera centering during the forced-kill/animation sequence to focus on the event (inferred).

reads.name: neverSinks
reads.likely_effect: Guard: when true, skip U8P_CLRLAVA::gotHit handling (prevents sinking/handling in certain global states).

writes.name: butthead
writes.likely_effect: Set to prevent reentrant handling while a forced-kill/animation sequence runs; used as a processing guard.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_CLRLAVA::gotHit is a guarded hit-handling process that responds when the object/NPC is struck. It prevents reentrancy (via butthead and neverSinks), inspects the hit source (param1), and branches on both param1 and the item's frame to either play a first-hit SFX/spawn a sprite and apply small randomized damage, destroy the source item, or run a forced-kill animation/sequence on an NPC.
